mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates continued labor market expansion that supports aggregate income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations remain firm.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or Spearman correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day return is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs for corporations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:effective fed funds rate at 4.94% in May 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy captured by a high federal funds rate and elevated consumer prices amplifies discount rate pressure and input cost burdens, jointly compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 4.94% and CPI at 201.9 in May 2006
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns, or returns are positive in more than half of joint-threshold events.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals diminishing manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, foreshadowing an earnings slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production index at 112.1 in May 2006
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns is above -0.03 during identified late-cycle windows, or positive returns exceed 50% in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1900 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and related downstream demand, reducing earnings for construction and financial sectors and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST falls below 1900, or positive returns occur in more than 45% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in payroll employment and retail sales indicates a broad consumer-led demand contraction that reduces corporate revenues across sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-declining episodes, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation captured by the consumer price index erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, creating downward pressure on the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days when the price level is rising.
failure_condition: Monthly changes in CPIAUCSL show a positive rank correlation with 5-day forward returns (IC > 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in less than 55% of rising-CPI months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects aggregate demand strength that supports near-term earnings revisions, with positive GDP momentum generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as macro surprises resolve into price discovery.
failure_condition: Quarterly changes in GDPC1 fail to positively predict 5-day forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of high-growth quarters.
